name: Artifact Synthesizer
description: Artifact generation and formatting module with clear separation between three concerns: PARSING (extracting structured data from LLM synthesis output), RENDERING (formatting prompt.md and constraints.yaml as strings), and WRITING (file I/O with overwrite control). Initialized with an output directory (defaults to cwd), the Synthesizer class provides the public API for generating, validating, displaying, and writing the two primary artifacts: an induced-understanding prompt (prompt.md) and a structured constraint set (constraints.yaml).

types.name: Severity
types.description: Constraint severity level. 'must' indicates a hard boundary that cannot be violated. 'should' indicates a strong preference that may be relaxed with justification.

types.name: Constraint

fields.name: id
fields.description: Sequential constraint identifier (e.g. C001). None before assignment.

fields.name: boundary
fields.description: The domain or area this constraint applies to (e.g. 'data-integrity', 'performance', 'security').

fields.name: condition
fields.description: The specific condition that must hold. A declarative statement of what is required.

fields.name: violation
fields.description: What constitutes a violation of this constraint — observable, testable criteria.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Whether this is a hard boundary ('must') or strong preference ('should').

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Why this constraint exists, grounded in the problem context from the interview.

types.description: A single structured constraint extracted from the LLM synthesis output. The id field is None when first parsed and assigned sequentially (C001, C002, ...) during rendering/serialization.

types.name: ConstraintId
types.description: Optional constraint identifier. None before assignment, 'CNNN' pattern after.

types.name: PromptSections

fields.name: system_context
fields.description: High-level description of the system, its purpose, stakeholders, and operational environment.

fields.name: consequence_map
fields.description: What happens when things go wrong — failure impacts mapped to stakeholders and business outcomes.

fields.name: failure_archaeology
fields.description: Known failure modes, past incidents, edge cases, and the lessons learned from them.

fields.name: dependency_landscape
fields.description: External systems, services, libraries, and assumptions the solution depends on.

fields.name: boundary_conditions
fields.description: Hard limits, non-negotiable requirements, regulatory constraints, and scope boundaries.

fields.name: success_shape
fields.description: What a correct, complete solution looks like — acceptance criteria and quality attributes.

types.description: The six required sections of the induced-understanding prompt (prompt.md). Each section contains markdown-formatted content written as a briefing to a senior engineer.

types.name: SynthesisInput

fields.name: raw_prompt_output
fields.description: Raw LLM output string containing the prompt sections as a JSON block with keys matching the six section names.

fields.name: raw_constraints_output
fields.description: Raw LLM output string containing constraint data as a JSON array of constraint objects.

types.description: The raw LLM output from the synthesis phase, expected to contain JSON-structured prompt sections and constraint data.

types.name: ArtifactPaths

fields.name: prompt_md_path
fields.description: Absolute path to the written prompt.md file as a string.

fields.name: constraints_yaml_path
fields.description: Absolute path to the written constraints.yaml file as a string.

types.description: Paths to the written artifact files on disk.

types.name: SynthesisError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message for display to the engineer.

fields.name: error_kind
fields.description: Categorized error type for programmatic handling.

types.description: Base error type for all synthesizer failures. Contains a human-readable message suitable for CLI display (not a stack trace).

types.name: SynthesisErrorKind
types.description: Categorized error types for synthesis failures.

types.name: OverwritePolicy
types.description: Policy for handling existing artifact files on disk.

fields.name: output_dir
fields.description: Directory path where artifacts will be written. Defaults to current working directory.

fields.name: overwrite_policy
fields.description: Policy for handling existing artifact files.

types.name: TerminalFormattedOutput

fields.name: content
fields.description: The formatted string content ready for terminal display.

fields.name: line_count
fields.description: Number of lines in the formatted output, for paging decisions.

types.description: Terminal-formatted string output with optional color/style hints.

functions.name: init_synthesizer
functions.description: Create and configure a Synthesizer instance with the given output directory and overwrite policy. Validates that the output directory exists and is writable.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Synthesizer configuration including output directory and overwrite policy.

error_cases.name: output_dir_not_found
error_cases.condition: config.output_dir does not exist

error_cases.name: output_dir_not_writable
error_cases.condition: config.output_dir exists but is not writable

preconditions: config.output_dir is a valid filesystem path
config.output_dir exists and is a directory
config.output_dir is writable by the current process
postconditions: Synthesizer instance is ready to accept parse/render/write calls
output_dir is stored as a resolved pathlib.Path

structured_side_effects.description: Checks existence and writability of the output directory.

functions.name: parse_prompt_sections
functions.description: Parse the raw LLM synthesis output for prompt.md into a structured PromptSections model. Expects the raw string to contain a JSON block (delimited by ```json ... ```) with keys: system_context, consequence_map, failure_archaeology, dependency_landscape, boundary_conditions, success_shape. Each value is a markdown string. Raises SynthesisError if JSON is malformed or any required section is missing.

inputs.name: raw_output
inputs.description: Raw LLM output string containing a JSON block with the six prompt section keys.

error_cases.name: no_json_block
error_cases.condition: raw_output does not contain a ```json ... ``` delimited block

error_cases.name: malformed_json
error_cases.condition: The JSON block cannot be parsed as valid JSON

error_cases.name: missing_section
error_cases.condition: One or more of the six required section keys is missing from the parsed JSON

error_cases.name: empty_section
error_cases.condition: One or more section values is empty or whitespace-only

error_cases.name: empty_input
error_cases.condition: raw_output is empty or whitespace-only

preconditions: raw_output is a non-empty string
raw_output contains a ```json ... ``` delimited block
postconditions: Returned PromptSections has all six section fields populated with non-empty strings
No file I/O or side effects occurred
Section content preserves original markdown formatting from LLM output

structured_side_effects.description: Pure parsing function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is raw_output length

functions.name: parse_constraints
functions.description: Parse the raw LLM synthesis output for constraints into a list of Constraint models. Expects the raw string to contain a JSON block (delimited by ```json ... ```) with an array of constraint objects, each having keys: boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale. The id field is NOT expected in the LLM output — IDs are assigned later during rendering. Validates each constraint via Pydantic model validation.

inputs.name: raw_output
inputs.description: Raw LLM output string containing a JSON block with an array of constraint objects.

error_cases.name: no_json_block
error_cases.condition: raw_output does not contain a ```json ... ``` delimited block

error_cases.name: malformed_json
error_cases.condition: The JSON block cannot be parsed as valid JSON

error_cases.name: not_array
error_cases.condition: The parsed JSON is not an array

error_cases.name: missing_field
error_cases.condition: A constraint object is missing one or more required fields (boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale)

error_cases.name: invalid_severity
error_cases.condition: A constraint has a severity value other than 'must' or 'should'

error_cases.name: empty_input
error_cases.condition: raw_output is empty or whitespace-only

error_cases.name: empty_array
error_cases.condition: The parsed JSON array contains zero constraints

preconditions: raw_output is a non-empty string
raw_output contains a ```json ... ``` delimited block
postconditions: All returned Constraint objects have boundary, condition, violation, severity, and rationale populated
All returned Constraint objects have severity equal to 'must' or 'should'
All returned Constraint objects have id set to None (not yet assigned)
Returned list preserves the order from the LLM output
No file I/O or side effects occurred

structured_side_effects.description: Pure parsing function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of constraints

functions.name: render_prompt_md
functions.description: Render a PromptSections model into the final prompt.md markdown string. Produces a document structured with H2 headers for each section (## System Context, ## Consequence Map, etc.) that reads like a briefing to a senior engineer. Includes a title header and brief preamble.

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: The validated prompt sections to render.

preconditions: sections has all six fields populated with non-empty strings
postconditions: Returned string starts with a markdown H1 title header
Returned string contains exactly six H2 section headers in order: System Context, Consequence Map, Failure Archaeology, Dependency Landscape, Boundary Conditions, Success Shape
Returned string is valid markdown
No file I/O or side effects occurred

structured_side_effects.description: Pure rendering function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total section content length

functions.name: render_constraints_yaml
functions.description: Render a list of Constraint models into the final constraints.yaml string. Assigns sequential IDs (C001, C002, ...) to constraints that have id=None. Uses pyyaml with a custom Dumper for multiline string block scalars and guaranteed field ordering (id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale). The output is a YAML list of constraint mappings.

inputs.name: constraints
inputs.description: The validated constraints to render. IDs will be assigned if not already set.

error_cases.name: empty_constraints
error_cases.condition: constraints list is empty

error_cases.name: duplicate_pre_assigned_ids
error_cases.condition: Two or more constraints have the same non-None id value

error_cases.name: yaml_serialization_failure
error_cases.condition: pyyaml fails to serialize the constraint data

preconditions: constraints is a non-empty list
All constraints have required fields populated (boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale)
postconditions: Returned string is valid YAML
All constraints in the YAML have unique IDs matching pattern C followed by three zero-padded digits
YAML field order per constraint is: id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale
Multiline string fields use YAML block scalar style (literal or folded)
No file I/O or side effects occurred
IDs are assigned sequentially starting from C001 for constraints with id=None

structured_side_effects.description: Pure rendering function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of constraints

functions.name: write_artifacts
functions.description: Write the rendered prompt.md and constraints.yaml strings to the configured output directory. Handles overwrite control according to the configured OverwritePolicy: 'error_if_exists' raises an error if either file exists, 'overwrite' silently overwrites, 'prompt_user' is handled by the caller (CLI layer) before invoking this function. Returns the absolute paths of the written files.

inputs.name: prompt_md
inputs.description: The rendered prompt.md content to write.

inputs.name: constraints_yaml
inputs.description: The rendered constraints.yaml content to write.

inputs.name: overwrite
inputs.description: If true, overwrite existing files without error. If false, raise error if files exist.

error_cases.name: prompt_md_exists
error_cases.condition: output_dir/prompt.md exists and overwrite is false

error_cases.name: constraints_yaml_exists
error_cases.condition: output_dir/constraints.yaml exists and overwrite is false

error_cases.name: write_io_error
error_cases.condition: Filesystem error during write (permissions, disk full, etc.)

error_cases.name: empty_prompt_md
error_cases.condition: prompt_md is empty or whitespace-only

error_cases.name: empty_constraints_yaml
error_cases.condition: constraints_yaml is empty or whitespace-only

preconditions: prompt_md is a non-empty string
constraints_yaml is a non-empty string
Synthesizer has been initialized with a valid, writable output_dir
If overwrite is false, caller has confirmed no existing files or has handled the prompt_user policy
postconditions: prompt.md file exists at output_dir/prompt.md with content equal to prompt_md
constraints.yaml file exists at output_dir/constraints.yaml with content equal to constraints_yaml
Both files are written with UTF-8 encoding
Returned ArtifactPaths contains the absolute paths of the written files

structured_side_effects.description: Writes the rendered prompt markdown to disk.
Writes the rendered constraints YAML to disk.

functions.name: check_existing_artifacts
functions.description: Check whether prompt.md and/or constraints.yaml already exist in the output directory. Used by the CLI layer to implement the prompt_user overwrite policy before calling write_artifacts.

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Filesystem error while checking for file existence

preconditions: Synthesizer has been initialized with a valid output_dir
postconditions: Returned ExistingArtifacts accurately reflects current filesystem state

functions.name: format_prompt_for_terminal
functions.description: Format the PromptSections for display in the terminal. Produces a human-readable, optionally color-enhanced representation suitable for CLI review. Truncates extremely long sections with a note about the full content being in the file.

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: The prompt sections to format for terminal display.

inputs.name: max_lines_per_section
inputs.description: Maximum number of lines to display per section before truncating.

preconditions: sections has all six fields populated
postconditions: Returned content is suitable for terminal display (no raw escape codes unless terminal supports them)
Sections longer than max_lines_per_section are truncated with a truncation notice
All six section headers are present in the output

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total section content length

functions.name: format_constraints_for_terminal
functions.description: Format a list of Constraints for display in the terminal. Produces a tabular or structured text representation showing each constraint's ID, boundary, condition, severity, and a truncated rationale. Suitable for CLI review or the 'constrain show' command.

inputs.name: constraints
inputs.description: The constraints to format for terminal display.

inputs.name: verbose
inputs.description: If true, show full rationale and violation fields. If false, show compact summary.

error_cases.name: missing_ids
error_cases.condition: One or more constraints have id=None

preconditions: All constraints have IDs assigned (non-None)
postconditions: Returned content is suitable for terminal display
Each constraint appears exactly once in the output
Constraints are displayed in ID order

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of constraints

functions.name: validate_constraints
functions.description: Perform synthesizer-level validation on a list of constraints beyond what Pydantic model validation provides. Checks for unique IDs (among those assigned), non-empty required fields, valid severity values, and overall list integrity. Returns the validated list or raises SynthesisError.

error_cases.name: empty_list
error_cases.condition: constraints list is empty

error_cases.name: duplicate_ids
error_cases.condition: Two or more constraints share the same non-None id

error_cases.name: invalid_severity
error_cases.condition: A constraint has severity not in {'must', 'should'}

error_cases.name: empty_required_field
error_cases.condition: A required string field (boundary, condition, violation, rationale) is empty or whitespace

error_cases.name: invalid_id_format
error_cases.condition: An assigned id does not match the CNNN pattern

preconditions: constraints is a non-empty list
postconditions: All returned constraints pass Pydantic model validation
All assigned IDs (non-None) are unique
All severity values are 'must' or 'should'
All required string fields are non-empty and non-whitespace

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation function with no side effects.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of constraints

functions.name: validate_prompt_sections
functions.description: Validate that a PromptSections model has all six sections present and non-empty. Performs content-level checks beyond Pydantic field presence.

error_cases.name: empty_section
error_cases.condition: One or more of the six section fields is empty or whitespace-only

error_cases.name: section_too_short
error_cases.condition: A section contains fewer than 10 characters, suggesting placeholder or stub content

postconditions: All six section fields are non-empty and non-whitespace strings
Returned sections are identical to input (pass-through on success)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation function with no side effects.

functions.name: synthesize
functions.description: Orchestrate the full synthesis pipeline: parse both raw LLM outputs, validate, render, and write artifacts. This is the primary high-level entry point used by the engine after the synthesis LLM call completes. Coordinates parse_prompt_sections -> validate_prompt_sections -> render_prompt_md, parse_constraints -> validate_constraints -> render_constraints_yaml -> write_artifacts in sequence. Returns the paths to written files.

inputs.name: synthesis_input
inputs.description: The raw LLM outputs from the synthesis phase.

inputs.name: overwrite
inputs.description: Whether to overwrite existing artifact files.

error_cases.name: prompt_parse_error
error_cases.condition: raw_prompt_output cannot be parsed into PromptSections

error_cases.name: constraints_parse_error
error_cases.condition: raw_constraints_output cannot be parsed into Constraint list

error_cases.name: validation_failure
error_cases.condition: Parsed data fails validation checks

error_cases.name: file_exists_no_overwrite
error_cases.condition: Artifact files exist and overwrite is false

error_cases.name: write_failure
error_cases.condition: File write fails due to I/O error

preconditions: Synthesizer has been initialized with a valid, writable output_dir
synthesis_input contains non-empty raw_prompt_output and raw_constraints_output
postconditions: prompt.md has been written to output_dir
constraints.yaml has been written to output_dir
Both files contain valid, well-formatted content
Returned ArtifactPaths contains absolute paths to both files

structured_side_effects.description: Writes the rendered prompt markdown to disk.
Writes the rendered constraints YAML to disk.

functions.name: assign_constraint_ids
functions.description: Assign sequential IDs (C001, C002, ...) to constraints that have id=None. Constraints with pre-assigned IDs are left unchanged. New IDs start after the highest existing ID in the list. Returns a new list with all IDs assigned.

inputs.name: constraints
inputs.description: Constraints to assign IDs to. May have some or all IDs as None.

error_cases.name: empty_list
error_cases.condition: constraints list is empty

error_cases.name: invalid_existing_id
error_cases.condition: A pre-assigned ID does not match the CNNN pattern

error_cases.name: duplicate_existing_ids
error_cases.condition: Two or more pre-assigned IDs are identical

error_cases.name: id_overflow
error_cases.condition: More than 999 constraints would require IDs beyond C999

preconditions: constraints is a non-empty list
Any pre-assigned IDs match the CNNN pattern
postconditions: All returned constraints have non-None IDs
All IDs match the CNNN pattern (C followed by three zero-padded digits)
All IDs are unique
Constraints with pre-assigned IDs retain their original IDs
New IDs are assigned sequentially starting after the highest pre-existing ID
Order of constraints is preserved

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function that returns a new list with IDs assigned.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of constraints

invariants: All Constraint IDs follow the pattern C followed by three zero-padded digits (e.g. C001, C002)
Constraint IDs are unique within any single list of constraints produced by the synthesizer
Constraint severity is always exactly 'must' or 'should'
PromptSections always contains all six required sections: system_context, consequence_map, failure_archaeology, dependency_landscape, boundary_conditions, success_shape
All file I/O uses pathlib.Path, never raw string paths
Rendering functions are pure — no side effects, no file I/O
Parsing functions are pure — no side effects, no file I/O
YAML output preserves field ordering: id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale
YAML output uses block scalar style for multiline string fields
prompt.md reads like a briefing to a senior engineer, not a requirements document
